title: festuca valesiaca subsp. wagneri
intro: Festuca valesiaca subsp. wagneri, also known as Wagner's fescue, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Festuca valesiaca subsp. wagneri include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote a healthy immune system.
